data.title: Update project docs to reflect language-neutral reviewer agent prompts and multi-stack dependency auditing
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Update CLAUDE.md and any other existing documentation that references Python-specific reviewer behavior or audit commands to reflect the polyglot reviewer system delivered by this epic.

**Why**: After Stories 1 and 2 land, any CLAUDE.md note or agent doc that describes ruff/mypy suppression behavior or pip-audit as the only dependency scanner will be stale.

**Scope**:
- IN: CLAUDE.md (reviewer behavior notes, if any); KNOWN-ISSUES.md (if any issues surfaced during implementation); any existing agent docs that reference the old Python-specific suppression rules
- OUT: Creating new docs; updating reviewer-base.md or REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md (Stories 1-2)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, no existing documentation describes ruff/mypy suppression in reviewer agents as Python-specific without noting the generic replacement
  ← Satisfies: documentation accuracy post-implementation
- When this story is complete, dependency audit documentation (if referenced in CLAUDE.md or DEPENDENCY-GUIDANCE.md references section) includes npm audit and bundle-audit

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Follow .claude/docs/DOCUMENTATION-GUIDE.md for formatting and conventions
- [Scope] Update only stale existing files — do not create new documentation

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
